title: Ploughman Singing
content:

content:
How oft on Sundays, when I'd time to tramp,
    My rambles led me to a gipsy's camp,
    Where the real effigy of midnight hags,

title: Twilight.
content:
The setting Sun withdraws his yellow light,
    A gloomy staining shadows over all,
    While the brown beetle, trumpeter of Night,

title: Spear Thistle
content:

title: Approach Of Winter
content:
The Autumn day now fades away,
     The fields are wet and dreary;
     The rude storm takes the flowers of May,
     And Nature seemeth weary

title: Ballad. "When Nature's Beauty Shone Complete."
content:
When nature's beauty shone complete.
    With summer's lovely weather,
    And even, shadowing day's retreat,
    Brought swains and maids toge

title: Song. "There Was A Time, When Love's Young Flowers"
content:
There was a time, when love's young flowers
    With many a joy my bosom prest:
    Sweet hours of bliss!--but short are hours,
    Those hours

title: To The Violet.
content:
Sweet tiny flower of darkly hue,
    Lone dweller in the pathless shade;
    How much I love thy pensive blue
    Of innocence so well display'

title: Maying; Or, A Love Of Flowers
content:
Upon a day, a merry day,
     When summer in her best,
     Like Sunday belles, prepares for play,
     And joins each merry guest,
     A ma

title: Early Love
content:
The Spring of life is o'er with me,
     And love and all gone by;
     Like broken bough upon yon tree,
     I'm left to fade and die.

title: Written In Autumn.
The Fear Of Flowers
content:
The nodding oxeye bends before the wind,
    The woodbine quakes lest boys their flowers should find,
    And prickly dogrose spite of its array

title: I Dreamt of Robin

    The daisies were quaking about in their white

Say, wilt thou go with me, sweet maid,

    Through the valley-depths of shade,

title: To Anna Three Years Old
content:
My Anna, summer laughs in mirth,

    And leave the crickets in the hearth
    For green fields' merry minstrel

title: Holywell.
content:
Nature, thou accept the song,
    To thee the simple lines belong,
    Inspir'd as brushing hill and dell
    I stroll'd the way to Holywell.

title: The Firetail's Nest
content:

    And then the bluecap tootles in its glee,

content:

     Now the pleasant Spring's begun.

content:

    And clumps of hawthorn shade;

title: Nature's Hymn To The Deity
content:

content:

title: Ballad by John Clare
content:
A faithless shepherd courted me,
He stole away my liberty.
When my poor heart was strange to men,
He came and smiled and stole it then.

When my apron

title: Song by John Clare
content:
Mary, leave thy lowly cot
 When thy thickest jobs are done;
When thy friends will miss thee not,
 Mary, to the pastures run.
Where we met the other ni

title: Summer Evening by John Clare
content:
The sinking sun is taking leave,
And sweetly gilds the edge of Eve,
While huddling clouds of purple dye
Gloomy hang the western sky.
Crows crowd croak

title: What is Life? by John Clare
content:
And what is Life?--An hour-glass on the run,
A mist retreating from the morning sun,
  A busy, bustling, still repeated dream;
Its length?--A minute's

title: The Maid Of Ocram or, Lord Gregory by John Clare
content:
Gay was the Maid of Ocram
As lady eer might be
Ere she did venture past a maid
To love Lord Gregory.
Fair was the Maid of Ocram
And shining like the s

title: The Ants
content:
What wonder strikes the curious, while he views

    Her woollen aprons and drab gowns
    For lady's cold array?
    And will

The eve put on her sweetest shroud,

    Freck'd with white and purple cloud,
    Dappled like a leopard's

title: The Lout
content:

    But plays at taw, and buys his Spanish juice.
    Hard as his toil, and ever slow to speak,

     And bluecaps so divinely blue, with poppies of bright scarlet hue,
     Maiden,

content:
Maid of the wilderness,
     Sweet in thy rural dress,
     Fond thy rich lips I press
     Under this tree.

     Morning her health bestows

content:
I love to see the old heath's withered brake

title: Memory
content:

     And pass away with every common lot:

content:

    Or by a hedge-row track, or padded balk;

    To go aboard a ship through love, and plough the ocean wide.

content:

     That poets oft have sung!--

-Solomon

content:

    To

    While timid hares were darting out,
    To crop the dewy flowers;

content:

    Earth's brightest gems of innocence repose.
    God is their parent, and they need no tear;
    H

Love

    Is yet not of approaches bold,
    Which gay dramatic tastes admi

content:
The wind waves oer the meadows green
    And shakes my own wild flowers

    Like the life of summer hours

content:
The Old Year's gone away
    To nothingness and night:
    We cannot find him all the day
    Nor hear him in the night:
    He left no footst

content:

    On the soft soundings of his village bells,
    As on a Sunday morning at his ease

content:

     And the river running free?
     There a maiden stood with me
     In Summer weather.

content:

    Clamours with dismal tidings of the rain,
    Roaring as rivers breaking loose in floods

Young Chloe looks sweet as the rose,

content:

title: The Sleep of Spring
content:

title: In Hilly-Wood
Life.
Where She Told Her Love
content:

    Where she broke the rose away

Signs of Winter

title: The Swallow
content:
Pretty swallow, once again
    Come and pass me in the rain.
    Pretty swallow, why so shy?
    Pass again my window by.

Written In November.
content:
Autumn, I love thy parting look to view

    When, thy life's dwindled thread worn nearly thro',

content:
Swamps of wild rush-beds, and sloughs' squashy traces,
    Grounds of rough fallows with thistle and weed,
    Flats and low vallies of kingcups

The Soldier

    Friends' letters coming from his native place

title: Childhood.
content:
What trifles touch our feelings, when we view
    The simple scenes of Childhood's early day,
    Pausing on spots where gather'd blossoms grew,

title: The Flitting
content:
I've left my own old home of homes,
    Green fields and every pleasant place;
    The summer like a stranger comes,

title: To Jane
content:
The lark's in the sky, love,
     The flowers on the lea,
     The whitethorn's in bloom, love,
     To please thee and me;
     'Neath its s

title: When Shall We Meet Again?
content:
How many times Spring blossoms meek
     Have faded on the land
     Since last I kissed that pretty cheek,
     Caressed that happy hand.

title: Summer Images
Poem On Death
content:
Why should man's high aspiring mind

        When all his haughty views can find

title: Stonepit
content:
The passing traveller with wonder sees
    A deep and ancient stonepit full of trees;

content:
The morning opens fine, bonny Mary O!

    Where the little Jenny wrens cock their tails among the h

title: My Bonny Alice And Her Pitcher
content:
There's a bonny place in Scotland,
     Where a little spring is found;
     There Nature shows her honest face
     The whole year round.

title: To The Lark
content:
Bird of the morn,
     When roseate clouds begin
     To show the opening dawn
     Thou gladly sing'st it in,
     And o'er the sweet green

title: Nature.
content:
O simple Nature, how I do delight
    To pause upon thy trifles--foolish things,
    As some would call them.--On the summer night,
    Tracing

title: The Disappointment.
content:

    As bearing her milk-burthen home:
    "Since he's broken his vow, near an hour has gone b

title: Recollections After A Ramble.
content:
The rosy day was sweet and young,
    The clod-brown lark that hail'd the morn
    Had just her summer anthem sung,
    And trembling dropped i

title: The Forest Maid
Among The Green Bushes
content:
Among the green bushes the songs of the thrushes
     Are answering each other in music and glee,
     While the magpies and rooks, in woods, h

title: Rural Evening.
Merry Maid
content:
Bonny and stout and brown, without a hat,
    She frowns offended when they call her fat--
    Yet fat she is, the merriest in the place,
    A

title: Decay
content:
O Poesy is on the wane,
    For Fancy's visions all unfitting;
    I hardly know her face again,
    Nature herself seems on the flitting.

title: To A Bower.
content:
Three times, sweet hawthorn! I have met thy bower,
    And thou hast gain'd my love, and I do feel
    An aching pain to leave thee: every flowe

content:

    Shines through the leafless shrubs all brown and grey,
    And smiles at winter be it eer so keen

title: Effusion.
content:
Ah, little did I think in time that's past,
    By summer burnt, or numb'd by winter's blast,
    Delving the ditch a livelihood to earn,
    O

title: Song's Eternity
content:
What is song's eternity?
    Come and see.
    Can it noise and bustle be?
    Come and see.
    Praises sung or praises said
    Can it be?

content:
I

Simple enchantress! wreath'd in summer blooms

To My Cottage.

    Yet where there's mischief he can find a way

The Fox

content:

     And everything is weariness to me,
     'T is a life without an end,

content:

content:
How pleasing simplest recollections seem!
    Now summer comes, it warms me to look back
    On the sweet happiness of youth's wild track,

title: May-Day.
content:
Now happy swains review the plains,
    And hail the first of May;
    Now linnets sing to welcome spring,
    And every soul is gay.

    Hob

title: The Face I Love So Dearly
content:
Sweet is the violet, th' scented pea,
     Haunted by red-legged, sable bee,
     But sweeter far than all to me
     Is she I love so dearly;

title: Love and Solitude
content:
I hate the very noise of troublous man
    Who did and does me all the harm he can.
    Free from the world I would a prisoner be

title: Farewell And Defiance To Love
content:
Love and thy vain employs, away
    From this too oft deluded breast!
    No longer will I court thy stay,
    To be my bosom's teazing guest.

title: On Taste.
content:
---------Taste is from heaven,
    An inspiration nature can't bestow;
    Though nature's beauties, where a taste is given,
    Warm the ideas

title: Rural Morning
content:
Soon as the twilight through the distant mist
    In silver hemmings skirts the purple east,
    Ere yet the sun unveils his smiles to view

title: Phoebe Of The Scottish Glen
content:
Agen I'll take my idle pen
     And sing my bonny mountain maid--
     Sweet Phoebe of the Scottish glen,
     Nor of her censure feel afraid.

title: Birds, Why Are Ye Silent?
content:
Why are ye silent, Birds?
     Where do ye fly?
     Winter's not violent,
     With such a Spring sky.
     The wheatlands are green, snow a

content:

     And pay the world no more regard,

    As

content:

        And live untith'd and free

    The hovel was ready to screen from a shower,

content:

title: Morning.

title: To Wordsworth
A Valentine
content:
Here's a valentine nosegay for Mary,
     Some of Spring's earliest flowers;
     The ivy is green by the dairy,
     And so are these laurels

title: Mary
content:

title: Native Scenes.
To Autumn.
Quail's Nest
content:
I wandered out one rainy day
    And heard a bird with merry joys
    Cry "wet my foot" for half the way;
    I stood and wondered at the noise

title: Mary Appleby
content:
I look upon the hedgerow flower,
     I gaze upon the hedgerow tree,
     I walk alone the silent hour,
     And think of Mary Appleby.

title: Now Is Past
content:
Now is past--the happy now
    When we together roved
    Beneath the wildwood's oak-tree bough
    And Nature said we loved.

content:

content:

title: To An Infant Daughter.
content:
Sweet gem of infant fairy-flowers!

title: To Jenny Lind
content:
I cannot touch the harp again,
     And sing another idle lay,
     To cool a maddening, burning brain,
     And drive the midnight fiend away

title: To A Dead Tree.
content:

title: A Specimen of Clare's rough drafts
content:

title: Woman.
Milking O' The Kye
content:
Young Jenny wakens at the dawn,
     Fresh as carnations newly blown,
     And o'er the pasture every morn
     Goes milking o' the kye.

title: Little Trotty Wagtail
content:
